behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module handoff (three collectors -> aggregation/payload builder -> summary step), not single-function logic. In-process fakes for the three sources and a spy/fake summary client can expose missing-source wiring bugs. Real OS/network/subprocess is not required, and correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with fake metrics API, fake issue-tracker client, and fake deployment-log reader that each return distinct sentinel values; assert the exact payload sent into summary generation contains all three sentinels and correct section mapping.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure is resilience/state propagation across modules (source failure handling -> report assembly), so Unit is too low. A fake source raising timeout/5xx and in-process wiring is enough to catch abort-vs-degrade logic. No real infrastructure is strictly required, and this behavior is about fallback control flow, not LLM quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with two healthy fake sources and one failing fake source; assert process completes, output report contains a `data unavailable` marker for only the failed section, and no global abort/uncaught exception occurs.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model's generated content quality/factuality, which a stub would mask. This is a single model-decision behavior (summary fidelity), not a multi-agent pipeline, so Workflow is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model using fixed input datasets with known key values; score output for factual inclusion/consistency of those values (with tolerance thresholds over repeated runs) and fail on omissions or contradictions.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery/inbox behavior (SMTP/API auth, routing, provider acceptance), which mocks can hide. This is infrastructure end-to-end validation; behavior text targets delivery success, not model-output quality, so Agentic is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end against real/staging source endpoints and real email delivery path to a test inbox; assert message is received by configured recipient, has expected subject/body structure, and includes report sections produced in that run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic because behavior is about inbox delivery correctness (`reaches ... inbox correctly`), not evaluating whether model narrative content is semantically good.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real failure mode at network/service boundary plus persistence across runs (filesystem state + subsequent run semantics). In-process mocks may miss real unreachable-error surfaces and retry-on-next-run behavior under actual process/runtime conditions. No model-output quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real delivery unreachability (e.g., blocked SMTP/API endpoint) during a run; assert report is persisted to disk. Restore delivery path and execute next scheduled run; assert persisted report is retried/sent and fallback artifact/state is cleared or marked delivered.
